description: Deep-review the 10 most critical ValidationForge skills (5 priority + 5 platform-routing). Read every SKILL.md plus its reference/workflow files line-by-line. Verify every command, every cross-reference, every PASS criterion. Fix broken instructions, stale references, and inaccurate PASS criteria. Document per-skill findings and consolidate into a summary report.

summary.title: Deep-review the 10 most critical ValidationForge skills
summary.one_line: Read every line of 10 priority SKILL.md files plus their reference/workflow files, verify cross-references and commands against the real plugin contents, fix every broken or stale instruction, and document per-skill findings.
deliverables: e2e-evidence/skill-review/summary.md — consolidated report ranking severity of findings
e2e-evidence/skill-review/fixes-applied.md — changelog of every SKILL.md / reference / workflow edit with rationale
Updated SKILL.md files and reference/workflow files for any broken instructions, stale references, or incorrect PASS criteria
non_goals: Do NOT review the other 31 skills (out of scope for this task).
Do NOT modify commands/, agents/, rules/, or hooks/ source — fix SKILL.md cross-references to match reality instead.
Do NOT create test files, mocks, or stubs of any kind (Iron Rule).

safe_parallel_work: All 10 skill reviews in Phase 1/2 touch different directories and can run in parallel.
Within a single skill's review subtask, reading SKILL.md + references/ + workflows/ is sequential.
must_be_sequential: Phase 3 (synthesis) must wait for Phase 1 + Phase 2 completion.
Phase 4 (fixes) must wait for Phase 3 consolidated severity ranking.
Phase 5 (re-validation) must wait for Phase 4 fixes to land.
parallelism_analysis.worker_split: Worker A: 5 priority skills (Phase 1). Worker B: 5 platform-routing skills (Phase 2). Both merge at Phase 3. 1 worker handles Phases 3/4/5 sequentially.

phases.name: Deep-review 5 priority skills
phases.description: For each of the 5 priority skills, read SKILL.md line-by-line; verify every referenced file exists at the cited path; read each reference file and check its commands/instructions for accuracy; trace cross-references to other skills, commands, rules, hooks; test representative instructions (tracing or actual execution when safe); produce a findings.md report. Evidence goes to e2e-evidence/skill-review/<skill>/.

subtasks.name: Review functional-validation skill
steps: Read SKILL.md and record each claim that can be verified (commands, file paths, cross-skill references).
Verify each references/*.md file exists; read each and check: (a) does every command in the file run? (b) do cited skills/commands exist? (c) are platform mentions current?
Cross-check the 4-step protocol: does it align with e2e-validate/workflows/execute.md?
Cross-check the platform detection table rows: do each of the 5 platform skills mentioned exist?
Write findings to e2e-evidence/skill-review/functional-validation/findings.md with sections: Summary, Accuracy Issues, Stale References, Missing Content, Broken Cross-Links, Recommendations.

verification.description: Reviewer rereads findings.md; at least one finding or an explicit 'no issues found' statement per section.

success_criteria: findings.md exists and covers every claim in SKILL.md
Every reference file was opened and inspected
subtasks.notes: Deep-reviewed functional-validation skill. Findings written to e2e-evidence/skill-review/functional-validation/findings.md (298 lines). 2 HIGH issues (team-adoption-guide.md .gitignore advice contradicts repo policy; quick-reference-card.md Android row references nonexistent skill), 4 MEDIUM (add Design row; manual HAR export; under-specified READ-evidence checklist; team-adoption-guide.md unlinked from SKILL.md), 3 LOW (platform-row order vs CLAUDE.md; Business Logic vs fullstack diagram; missing sequential-analysis in Related Skills). No CRITICAL defects — no finding would cause a false PASS verdict. All 10 cross-skill links verified to resolve. All 4 reference files opened and inspected. block-test-files hook existence and allowlist verified.

subtasks.name: Review gate-validation-discipline skill
steps: Read SKILL.md; list each step of the verification loop.
Open references/evidence-standards.md; validate each Good/Bad example still matches current evidence format.
Open references/gate-integration-examples.md; verify every example command runs and references real commands/skills.
Check that cross-references to no-mocking-validation-gates, functional-validation, and any hooks are accurate (open hooks/ to confirm).
Write e2e-evidence/skill-review/gate-validation-discipline/findings.md.

verification.description: Reviewer confirms every anti-pattern in evidence-standards.md is still a relevant pitfall.

subtasks.notes: Deep-reviewed gate-validation-discipline skill. Findings written to e2e-evidence/skill-review/gate-validation-discipline/findings.md (263 lines). 1 HIGH issue (stale term "Ultrawork" in gate-integration-examples.md — not used anywhere else in the repo; should be "Team mode"), 2 MEDIUM (gate-integration-examples.md evidence-path convention contradicts rules/team-validation.md subdirectory-per-validator rule; CI-example flat filenames not acknowledged as exception to journey-slug pattern), 3 LOW (informal "orchestrator" term vs canonical "Lead"/validation-lead agent; missing parallel-validation from Related Skills; overlap with verification-before-completion not disambiguated). No CRITICAL defects — no finding would cause a false PASS verdict. All 4 cross-skill links verified reciprocal. All 10 anti-pattern rows verified still relevant. All 5 evidence-type Good/Bad examples verified accurate. CI-example tooling verified (curl/jq/pnpm/npx playwright syntax all valid on this machine).

subtasks.name: Review no-mocking-validation-gates skill
steps: Read SKILL.md; confirm Iron Rule language and blocked pattern table.
Open mock-pattern-catalog.md; verify every listed file pattern is actually blocked by ./hooks/block-test-files (read the hook source).
Confirm the 'What Is NOT Blocked' allowlist matches the hook's allowlist.
Verify every code pattern example is syntactically valid for its language.
Open real-system-validation-guide.md; verify commands, example mock-drift case, and cross-skill references.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/no-mocking-validation-gates/findings.md.

verification.description: Cross-verify against hooks/block-test-files (or equivalent hook script): does SKILL.md claim a pattern is blocked that the hook does not block? Record any mismatch as a finding.

success_criteria: SKILL.md claims vs hook behavior gap documented
Every language in the catalog was checked for syntax accuracy
subtasks.notes: Deep-reviewed no-mocking-validation-gates skill. Findings in e2e-evidence/skill-review/no-mocking-validation-gates/findings.md. 3 HIGH issues (SKILL.md/catalog claim __mocks__/ blocked but no pattern matches; catalog claims *_test.py blocked but only test_*.py is; catalog claims Java/Kotlin/Rust test files blocked but no patterns exist for them), 5 MEDIUM (JS/Python/Swift/Go/Rust code-pattern sections overclaim — only a subset of patterns are in MOCK_PATTERNS: jest.fn/vi.fn/@patch/httptest.NewServer/func Test etc. all NOT detected — including a case-sensitivity bug where /func test.*\(\)/ matches lowercase while Go convention is TestXxx), 5 LOW (Rule 2 wording implies PreToolUse blocking but mock-detection is PostToolUse; minor wording issues). No CRITICAL defects. All 4 Related Skills cross-links verified. All 4 ALLOWLIST entries verified 1:1 with hook source. Empirical evidence: ran test-patterns.js against hooks/patterns.js with 28 file paths + 42 code patterns; transcript saved (74 lines). Recorded follow-up TODO to expand MOCK_PATTERNS (outside this task's scope — no hook source edits per spec).

subtasks.name: Review preflight skill
steps: Read SKILL.md; confirm report format, severity levels, and the 6 rules.

Open platform-checklists.md; for each row (Web/API/iOS/CLI/Fullstack): run the check command on this machine where safe (node --version, pnpm --version, xcrun --version); record actual output; flag any command that errors or gives misleading output.
Open auto-fix-actions.md; for each auto-fix: confirm command is idempotent and safe to run in the sandbox; flag any 'brew install' or 'sudo' that should be manual-only.
Verify the platform detection bash script in auto-fix-actions.md correctly handles the 5 platforms.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/preflight/findings.md.

verification.description: Execute at least the Web and API platform checklists from platform-checklists.md against this worktree; record output in findings.md.

success_criteria: At least 10 checklist commands executed and outputs recorded
Auto-fix safety review performed for each row
subtasks.notes: Deep-reviewed preflight skill. Findings in e2e-evidence/skill-review/preflight/findings.md (317 lines). 1 CRITICAL issue (platform-detection bash script uses `[ -d "*.xcodeproj" ]` with quoted glob — never matches real iOS projects; empirically confirmed in platform-detection-test.txt Test 2 where MyApp.xcodeproj dir detects as 'unknown'), 2 HIGH (`ls node_modules/.package-lock.json` Dependencies check fails for pnpm/yarn/bun projects; `xcode-select --install` auto-fix row directly contradicts Rule 3 "NEVER auto-fix by installing major tools"), 7 MEDIUM (hardcoded postgresql@16, iPhone 15/16 simulator names, Fullstack has no command table unlike other 4 platforms, PASS/FAIL/WARN↔severity mapping never bound, .env check ignores .env.local/.env.development, Design platform missing from checklist+detection script, docker-compose precedence shadowed by next.config.js), 5 LOW (sleep 3 vs condition-based-waiting inconsistency, jq brew install gray-area under Rule 3, destructive migration auto-fix unguarded, PORT template literal, report-example check-count inconsistency). No CRITICAL mock/test-file creation. All 5 Related Skills verified reciprocal. 19 checklist commands executed across Web/API/iOS/CLI with transcripts saved. Auto-fix safety reviewed for 9 of 12 rows (3 are trivial templates). Evidence files: web-checklist-output.txt, api-checklist-output.txt, ios-checklist-output.txt, cli-checklist-output.txt, platform-detection-test.txt, env-file-check.txt, deps-check-analysis.txt, autofix-review.txt, simctl-devices.txt, crossref-check.txt.

subtasks.name: Review e2e-validate skill (orchestrator)
steps: Read SKILL.md; list every workflow and reference cited.
Confirm every workflows/<name>.md and references/<name>.md file exists; flag missing ones.
Read each workflow file (plan.md, execute.md, etc.); verify commands, PASS criteria generation, and delegation to platform skills.
Read each platform reference file under references/; verify content matches the corresponding skills/<platform>-validation/SKILL.md (no contradictions).
Verify the orchestrator correctly sequences the 7 pipeline phases from CLAUDE.md.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/e2e-validate/findings.md.

verification.description: Reviewer confirms every file link resolves and every cross-skill delegation is accurate.

success_criteria: All 6 platform references cross-checked against their platform skill
Contradictions between orchestrator and delegated skills listed
subtasks.notes: Largest skill — allocate the most reading time here.

phases.name: Deep-review 5 platform-routing skills
phases.description: For each platform-routing skill, trace the numbered Steps 1..N; verify every command syntax; verify MCP tool names (Playwright MCP / Chrome DevTools MCP / iOS simulator MCP); verify PASS criteria templates and Common Failures troubleshooting tables. Each skill is standalone (no references/ dir), so the review fits in one subtask per skill.

subtasks.name: Review ios-validation skill
steps: Read all 9 Steps and PASS criteria template.
Verify every xcodebuild, xcrun simctl, and idb command: does xcrun simctl --help recognize the flags listed? Note: do NOT actually boot simulators unless a simulator is already present and safe.
Confirm Common Failures table still describes current Xcode 15/16 behavior.
Cross-check Related Skills section against ./skills/ listing.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/ios-validation/findings.md.

verification.description: Run at least xcodebuild -version and xcrun simctl list devices (read-only) and record output.

success_criteria: PASS criteria template reviewed for completeness

subtasks.name: Review web-validation skill
steps: Read all 8 Steps (dev server, health, navigation, console, network, form, responsive, routes).

Verify every Playwright MCP tool name (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests, browser_fill_form, browser_resize) matches current Playwright MCP server.
Verify every Chrome DevTools MCP tool name (navigate_page, take_snapshot, take_screenshot, click) matches current server.
Check dev-server detection script for pnpm/npm/yarn/manage.py/Gemfile — does it handle bun/deno/vite? Flag missing cases.
Check responsive viewport table — are the 4 viewports still canonical?
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/web-validation/findings.md.

verification.description: Reviewer traces all 8 steps; flags any MCP tool name that no longer exists in the current MCP server spec.

success_criteria: Every MCP tool mentioned verified to exist in current server

subtasks.name: Review api-validation skill
steps: Read all 6 Steps (CRUD, auth, error paths, perf).
Verify every curl invocation: correct flags, jq filter syntax, and HTTP semantics.
Verify auth flow covers bearer/API-key/cookie cases.
Check that evidence capture instructions produce non-empty files.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/api-validation/findings.md.

verification.description: Run curl --version and jq --version and a sample curl to a public endpoint to confirm syntax works.

subtasks.name: Review cli-validation skill
steps: Read all 8 Steps (build, help, version, exec, exit codes, stdin, stdout/stderr, evidence).
Verify build commands for Rust (cargo), Go (go build), Python (python -m), Node (npm/bin) still match current tooling.
Verify exit-code handling across platforms (macOS vs Linux differences).
Check that PASS criteria template covers all 8 steps.
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/cli-validation/findings.md.

verification.description: Run cargo --version || true; go version || true; python3 --version to record tooling baseline.

subtasks.name: Review fullstack-validation skill
steps: Read all Steps; confirm bottom-up ordering (DB -> API -> Frontend -> Integration).
Verify delegation to api-validation and web-validation is explicit and consistent.
Check DB readiness checks for PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite.
Confirm Common Failures covers cross-layer race conditions (e.g., frontend starts before API is ready).
Write findings.md to e2e-evidence/skill-review/fullstack-validation/findings.md.

verification.description: Reviewer traces one full bottom-up sequence; records any step that contradicts api-validation or web-validation.

success_criteria: No contradictions with api-validation or web-validation

phases.name: Synthesize consolidated review report
phases.description: Aggregate the 10 per-skill findings.md files into one summary that ranks findings by severity and proposes a fix plan.

subtasks.name: Write consolidated summary
steps: Read every per-skill findings.md.
Build a severity-ranked issue table: CRITICAL (instruction leads to false verdict), HIGH (stale reference or broken link), MEDIUM (missing content, unclear wording), LOW (nice-to-have improvement).
List fix recommendations grouped by file (one section per skill).
Write ./e2e-evidence/skill-review/summary.md.

verification.description: Summary covers all 10 skills and every issue in the per-skill findings.

success_criteria: Every per-skill finding mapped into summary

phases.name: Apply fixes to SKILL.md and reference files
phases.description: Apply every CRITICAL/HIGH fix surfaced by the synthesis. MEDIUM/LOW fixes applied if time permits; otherwise logged for follow-up. Every edit recorded in fixes-applied.md with rationale and cited evidence (source line in findings).

subtasks.name: Apply fixes in priority order
steps: For each CRITICAL issue from summary.md, edit the affected file with the Edit tool; record the change in fixes-applied.md with before/after excerpts.
Repeat for HIGH issues.
For MEDIUM/LOW issues, apply if quick; otherwise note as deferred in fixes-applied.md.
If a reference file is completely stale, rewrite its content rather than deleting it.
NEVER edit commands/, agents/, rules/, hooks/ source — if a SKILL.md references a nonexistent command/hook, fix the SKILL.md, not the missing asset.

verification.description: Every CRITICAL/HIGH issue from summary.md has a matching entry in fixes-applied.md with a commit-ready diff description.

success_criteria: No test files, mocks, or stubs created (Iron Rule preserved)
YAML frontmatter intact in every SKILL.md after edits

phases.name: Re-validate fixed skills
phases.description: For every skill with applied fixes, re-read the now-updated SKILL.md and re-trace the instruction that was broken. Confirm the fix actually resolves the issue without regressing another claim.

subtasks.name: Re-trace fixed instructions
steps: For each row in fixes-applied.md, re-open the affected file and confirm the new wording/command is correct.
Run any check-command that was failing before (where safe); confirm new output matches the claim.
Update summary.md with a 'Resolved' column stating which issues are closed.
Finalize e2e-evidence/skill-review/summary.md.

verification.description: All CRITICAL and HIGH rows in summary.md have Resolved=Yes or a deferred explanation.

success_criteria: summary.md updated with resolution status for every issue
No regressions introduced by the fix phase

evidence_requirements: fixes-applied.md with before/after excerpts for every edit
Command transcripts under e2e-evidence/skill-review/<skill>/ (e.g., node-version.txt, xcrun-simctl-list.txt)
gate_checks: gate-validation-discipline: every claim in this plan's fixes-applied.md must cite evidence (a line from findings.md).
no-mocking-validation-gates: no test files, mocks, or stubs created at any point.
preflight: before Phase 4 fixes, preflight report captured (node/xcode/tooling versions).
verification_strategy.notes: Risk is medium (not high) because we are editing Markdown, not code. But the downstream impact on every /validate run is significant, so discipline around evidence citation is mandatory.

criteria: All 10 priority skills reviewed (findings.md exists for each).
Every reference/workflow file cited by those skills was opened and verified to exist.
Every CRITICAL and HIGH issue in summary.md has a matching fix row in fixes-applied.md or a documented deferral.
No SKILL.md YAML frontmatter was broken.
No files outside ./skills/ and ./e2e-evidence/skill-review/ were modified (except this spec dir).
Iron Rule preserved: no test files, mocks, or stubs created.

qa_sign_off.tests_passed: n/a (markdown review task; verification is manual + command transcripts)
